frame_analyses.description: Surgeon holding red suture with both hands, beginning extracorporeal knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good initial suture positioning, proper tension maintained

frame_analyses.description: Creating first loop in extracorporeal knot, suture being manipulated with precise hand movements
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean loop formation technique, appropriate working length

frame_analyses.description: Continuing knot formation with multiple wraps, creating complex knot structure
Further development of knot with continued wrapping technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent tension maintained throughout wrapping process

frame_analyses.description: Finalizing extracorporeal knot structure before advancement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears well-formed and ready for pusher advancement

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being advanced to deliver knot to tissue level
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot pusher technique, steady advancement

confidence_rationale: Video shows only extracorporeal knot tying portion without clear view of Penrose drain, suture placement marks, or complete task sequence. Unable to assess critical components like suture placement accuracy, drain condition, or full task completion.
cannot_determine: Suture placement through marks on Penrose drain - not visible in frames
Exact deviation from placement marks - drain not shown clearly
Gap in drain after suturing - drain position not visible
Complete task sequence including needle loading and suture cutting
Whether knot was successfully pushed to appropriate tissue level
technique_summary: Excellent extracorporeal knot formation technique with expert-level timing (18 seconds total). However, this appears to be only a demonstration of the knot tying portion rather than a complete FLS Task 4 performance.
strengths: Very fast and efficient extracorporeal knot formation
Excellent hand coordination and suture control
improvement_suggestions: Complete assessment requires full task sequence including suture placement and cutting phases
